name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram operates a global network of money transfer locations, including partner retailers like Walmart, CVS, and local shops, enabling customers to send and receive funds domestically and internationally.
description_long:
MoneyGram is a well-established money transfer service that operates through a distributed network of physical locations rather than as a standalone storefront. The company partners with major retailers and local businesses to provide convenient access to their services across Kansas City and beyond. Their model relies on existing commercial locations—supermarkets, pharmacies, liquor stores, and specialty shops—to serve as agent locations where customers can conduct transactions.

MoneyGram's core service is facilitating domestic and international money transfers through both online and in-person channels. Customers can send money to recipients in other locations and receive incoming transfers at any of MoneyGram's partner locations. The service also includes money orders and bill payment capabilities. The platform emphasizes convenience by allowing customers to manage transfers online or visit a nearby physical location during business hours, with partner stores typically open until 6 PM to 11:59 PM depending on the location.

What distinguishes MoneyGram is its extensive retail partnership network. Rather than operating their own branches, they leverage established businesses—particularly Walmart, CVS, and various independent retailers—to maximize geographic accessibility. In Kansas City alone, the search results show 26 available locations, including multiple Walmart stores, Ace Cash Express locations, and neighborhood shops. This partnership model allows them to maintain lower overhead while reaching customers through familiar, trusted retailers.

MoneyGram operates in a competitive market alongside Western Union and other transfer services. While the distributed network provides convenience, customers are dependent on partner store hours and locations. The service is most practical for customers who already shop at or frequent these partner locations. International transfer rates and fees are not disclosed on this location-finder page, requiring customers to contact individual locations or visit the main website for pricing details.
pros: 26 search results showing extensive location coverage in Kansas City area, including major retailers like Walmart and CVS
Multiple convenient operating hours, with some locations open until 11:59 PM (Pronto Stop Liquor)
Both online and offline transfer management options available
Partnership with recognizable national retailers (Walmart, CVS) and local businesses increases accessibility
Ability to send and receive money domestically and internationally through same network
Money order and bill payment services available in addition to transfers
Available at both large chain stores and neighborhood-focused locations (Latino Stop, El Centenario, La Jarochita)
cons: Limited by partner store hours—some locations close as early as 6 PM (CVS), making after-hours transfers impossible
Fees and exchange rates not displayed on location finder page; requires contacting individual locations
Dependent on third-party retailers for service delivery, which may limit consistent service quality
No dedicated MoneyGram storefronts in results—all locations are agent-based, removing direct company accountability
Transfer speed not mentioned on this page, and timing may vary by location or transfer type
best_for: Customers who frequently shop at Walmart, CVS, or partner retailers and want convenient transfer access
Individuals sending money domestically or internationally who prefer in-person transactions over fully digital services
Kansas City residents needing basic money order and bill payment services alongside transfer options
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for customers who want convenient, in-person access to money transfers through familiar retailers like Walmart and CVS, with the flexibility of both online management and walk-in service. The main caveat is that users are limited by partner store hours and locations, and pricing information requires direct contact rather than being transparently displayed online.
services: In-person money transfer at physical locations
Agent location network across retail partners
